Before AI can deliver value, people need to trust the information it runs on.

That trust doesn’t come from a model alone. It comes from strong information management: content that is connected, records that are governed, and knowledge that is easy to find and use.

That’s why the AI+IM Global Summit is so timely. The event brings together information leaders focused on a practical question many organizations are still working through: how do we pair AI with the right foundation so it improves decisions, supports adoption, and creates measurable results?

This is not just about new technology. It’s about building workflows that people can rely on and scaling growth without creating more complexity.

For leaders shaping AI strategy in 2026, the opportunity is clear: get the information layer right, and the rest gets more achievable.

A useful AI strategy starts with an honest question:

Is your information ready?

For many organizations, the real challenge is not access to AI tools. It is the condition of the content, records, and knowledge those tools depend on. If information is scattered or unmanaged, AI initiatives become harder to trust, harder to scale, and harder to connect to measurable value.

The AI+IM Global Summit focuses on that critical intersection between AI and information management. It is designed for leaders who want practical insight into how unstructured information can support smarter decisions, stronger governance, and more effective automation.

What stands out is the focus on real impact. Not hype. Not generic transformation language. Just a clearer path to making AI useful, usable, and aligned to the way organizations actually work.

For information leaders, operations leaders, and anyone shaping AI strategy, this is a meaningful opportunity to learn from peers and strengthen the foundation before scaling further.
